title: Multi-hop ownership-chain scoping for school lookups
business_reason: One-hop parent scoping (FR-0048) covered tables one FK from a school-scoped parent. Generalize to a multi-hop ownership chain so a table several FKs deep (e.g. result_batches -> score_batches -> import_batches[school_id]) is also scoped — keeping zero cross-school leak.

design.discovery: gen_ui.emit_school_scope replaces the one-hop 'parent' with a BFS 'path' of ownership FKs (<=3 hops) to the nearest school-scoped table. Edges through actor/staff/created_by/updated_by/reviewer/etc. are EXCLUDED, so the chain never scopes by an incidental or staff-exposing relationship (the same hazard that ruled out auto-junctions).
design.result: one-hop tables now emit a 1-hop path (unchanged behavior); result_batches gains a verified 2-hop chain; nothing previously scoped was lost.
design.endpoint: /api/school/lookup resolves a path by starting at the school-scoped ancestor (filtered by the authenticated school_id) and walking each hop down, narrowing the id set, until the target table — never trusting the browser's school_id.

param_14.question: Are multi-hop relationships scoped to the school?
param_14.answer: Yes. The scope map now carries an ownership-FK chain (1+ hops) per table; the endpoint walks it from the school-scoped ancestor down to the target, so a table several FKs deep (e.g. result_batches via its score-batch and import-batch) is scoped to the school. The chain follows only ownership FKs (actor/audit edges excluded), so it can't scope by a staff-exposing relationship. Verified no-leak and fail-closed: the seed's global result_batches aren't chain-linked to any school, so they are shown to none.
param_14.security_implications: chain walks only ownership FKs (incidental/actor/audit edges excluded); each hop is filtered by the school's own ids; unchained records are shown to no school (fail-closed); browser school_id never trusted.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: positive multi-hop demo needs a fully-chained result_batch in the seed.

verify: live: result_batches chain excludes the 3 global (unchained) rows -> 0 for every school (fail-closed)
